text.title: Eval-Driven Development for AI Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules implementing the TDD discipline for AI features. Every rule has a binary check, a rationale, and an attributable source.

rule.statement: This methodology applies when a PR touches prompt, prompt-rendering code, model configuration, retrieval, tool schemas, or system messages. Non-AI PRs are exempt.
rule.rationale: Methodology overhead must align with risk surface. Applying TDD-for-AI to a CSS PR wastes cycles and erodes trust in the gate.

rule.statement: PRs in scope MUST include an eval-delta line in the description: stratum-level before/after on the relevant suite plus a one-sentence interpretation. PRs without an eval-delta are reopened, not silently approved.
rule.rationale: Without a forced delta line in the PR, regressions on minority strata (the persona that breaks while the average improves) stay invisible until customers report them.

rule.statement: CI fails the merge when the regression on any stratum exceeds the configured threshold. "Looks fine in dev" does not override the gate.

rationale.source: Eval gating research; Schuster et al. 2023

rule.rationale: Local-only evals get skipped under deadline pressure, which is precisely when regressions ship. CI gates make the eval suite a discipline, not a dashboard.

rule.statement: The eval suite has explicit drift watch: monthly review checks that scoring functions still discriminate (not saturated at 100%), strata still cover real production traffic, and judge models have not silently changed. Suites that lose discrimination on >30% of cases are sunset and rebuilt rather than patched indefinitely.

rationale.source: Drift research; HELM benchmark retirement notes

rule.rationale: Evals that everyone passes are no longer evals; they are decoration. A sunset criterion prevents teams from carrying a green suite that has stopped meaning anything — the worst form of false confidence.

rule.statement: When stratum gate passes, eval-delta is recorded, and no incident is open against the affected feature, the merge is approved automatically.
rule.rationale: Decision-tree conclusions must reference an existing rule. r5 captures the happy-path exit so the tree resolves cleanly without dangling refs.

rule.statement: Skip when Applies-If conditions are not met (no AI surface in the PR; prototype with no shipping plan; deterministic-only feature).
rule.rationale: Decision tree must have a clean skip path so non-applicable inputs do not produce false-positive verdicts.
